excluded_defs.name: log_it_
excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm it has the single tracer_.push_back call; confirmed it is the sole place after the fix

exploration_log.aha_moment: sink_it_() is called from log_it_() when log_enabled=true. If sink_it_() also pushes to the tracer, then for every normally-logged message with backtrace enabled, the message occupies two slots in the circular_q: once from sink_it_() and once from the unconditional push in log_it_(). The fix is simple: remove the redundant push from sink_it_().
exploration_log.hindsight: The test suite's bactrace1 test still passes even with the double-push (because the buffer overflow hides it), making this a subtle capacity bug.

alternatives: Add a guard in log_it_ to skip push when log_enabled is true
key_decisions.decision: Remove the tracer_.push_back from sink_it_(), leaving log_it_ as sole pusher
key_decisions.reasoning: The task description explicitly says to remove from sink_it_(); log_it_ is the correct semantic owner of the backtrace decision

solve_notes: Removed the redundant `should_backtrace()` / `tracer_.push_back()` call from `sink_it_()` in `logger-inl.h`. When a message passes the logger-level check (`log_enabled=true`) and backtrace is enabled, `log_it_()` already calls `tracer_.push_back()` unconditionally. The additional push in `sink_it_()` caused each normally-logged message to consume two slots in the circular_q, halving the effective backtrace capacity.
task_text:
Fix backtracer double-pushing messages when normal logging is enabled

The `logger` class in `logger-inl.h` pushes messages to the backtrace
ring buffer in two separate places: once inside `sink_it_()` (via the
`should_backtrace()` check at the bottom of that method) and once
directly in `log_it_()` (via the unconditional `tracer_.push_back`
call when `traceback_enabled` is true). When a message passes the
logger-level check (`log_enabled=true`), both code paths execute,
causing the message to consume two slots in the `circular_q` inside
`details/backtracer.h`. This halves the effective backtrace capacity
for normally-logged messages. Fix `logger::sink_it_()` in `logger.h`
by removing the `should_backtrace()` / `tracer_.push_back()` call from
it, leaving `log_it_()` as the sole place that enqueues messages into
the backtrace.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Creates logger with test_sink and calls logger.info/debug; exercises sink_it_() directly and through the double-push path introduced by the bug

thrash_preventing_defs.name: log_it_
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms log_it_ is the existing sole caller of both sink_it_() and tracer_.push_back(); prevents agent from incorrectly thinking sink_it_ needs to keep the push for correctness

thrash_preventing_defs.name: should_backtrace
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms should_backtrace() just returns tracer_.enabled(); prevents agent from needing to search for its definition separately

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just sink_it_() where the edit happens. The thrash_preventing set adds log_it_() (to confirm it has the authoritative tracer_.push_back call) and should_backtrace() (to confirm it's a trivial wrapper around tracer_.enabled()). An AI agent would read log_it_() to understand the call graph before modifying sink_it_(), and would look up should_backtrace() to verify the condition.
